//: Tiny Tapeout 1x1 digital tile hardening for the gf180 64x8 SRAM macro.
Modeled on urish/ttihp-sram-test; the macro nearly fills the tile so most settings are
about fitting + connecting the macro rather than placing much logic.

instances.//: Flip vertically (FS) so the macro's signal pins — all on its bottom edge — end
up at the TOP, next to the tile's top-edge I/O pins. With orientation N the
signals were on the bottom edge and every net had to cross the full 152um macro
height on Metal4 (the only free layer), badly congesting routing. For FS the origin
maps to the macro's top-left, so location y = bbox top (4.255 + 152.21 = 156.465).

//: Connect the tile core nets (VPWR/VGND) to the macro's VDD/VSS power pins.
The macro nearly fills the tile; don't let the resizer insert per-input delay buffers
(16 of them) — there is no room to legally place them in the thin side margins.
gf180 SRAM power pins are on Metal4 = the PDN vertical layer; stripes overlap the pins
and connect by net name (see pdn_cfg.tcl cmos5l branch).
!!! DO NOT CHANGE ANYTHING BELOW THIS POINT (TT SRAM-project conventions) !!!
Metal4 is the ONLY free layer over the macro (it blocks M1-M3), shared between the
PDN and all signal routing to the macro's bottom-edge pins. Keep the PDN sparse so
signals have room — a dense grid caused signal<->VPWR shorts on Metal4. Even a few
straps still connect the macro's (internally-bussed) Metal3 power fingers.
